summary: Completed comprehensive backend API testing for SKAPA Spotify OAuth PKCE authentication system. All 24 tests passed covering health endpoint, validation, authentication, session management, encryption, JWT structure, MongoDB indexes, and CORS configuration.
critical_code_review_comments: Code is well-structured with proper separation of concerns (auth.py, me.py, security.py, db.py, config.py)
AES-256-GCM encryption properly implemented with random nonce for refresh tokens
JWT tokens correctly use HS256 with jti claim and 30-day expiry
MongoDB indexes properly configured for users.spotify_id (unique), users.handle (unique sparse), sessions.jti (unique), sessions.expires_at (TTL)
CORS configured to allow all origins as expected
Session revocation properly implemented - logout invalidates session and subsequent requests return 401
seed_data_creation: Test users with prefix 'TEST_' created and cleaned up during authenticated flow tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs working correctly. Test files created in /app/backend/tests/ can be reused for regression testing. Spotify OAuth callback cannot be fully tested without real OAuth code - tests simulate post-OAuth state by directly inserting users and minting JWTs.
mocked_api_note: Spotify token exchange is MOCKED - cannot test full OAuth round-trip without browser. Tests simulate post-callback state by directly upserting users and minting JWTs via backend's security module.
